ground_truth.title: Step by Step Guide to Mobile App Development Process - Velvetech
ground_truth.author: Matthew Carrington
ground_truth.main_content:
Every day, the number of apps released to the market grows. Plus, right now, there are more mobile devices in the world than there are people. No wonder that by 2030 mobile applications are projected to generate $626.39 billion in revenue. With such promising forecasts, it's easy to see why many companies are looking into mobile app development. Some businesses are interested in creating the next TikTok or Zoom. Thus, focusing on consumer-facing projects and the revenue they can generate. Others want to optimize internal processes and choose to invest in enterprise solutions that can reduce expenses and improve efficiencies.

The 8 Key Steps of Mobile App Development Process: The process of mobile app development can somewhat vary on an individual basis. However, the following eight steps are the ones that you'll likely have to go through:

Step 1: Strategy Development - The very first step of the app development process is, unsurprisingly, defining the strategy. At this point, you've got to start thinking carefully about your future application, its goals, capabilities, and business model. This involves identifying objectives, researching competitors, selecting platform (Android, iOS, or cross-platform), and choosing monetization method.

Step 2: Analysis and Planning - The second step to take is analysis and planning. At this point, you already have your idea in mind, have done some research, and know the general direction of where you're heading. Now is the time to get practical and turn to business analysis services to identify all of the details you need before jumping into the design and development stages. This includes functional and non-functional requirements, product roadmap, technology stack, project estimation, and software development methodology.

Step 3: UI/UX Design - In order for users to get the best experience, a lot of attention has to be paid to an app's design. If it turns out messy or faulty, users will immediately flock to competitors and abandon the tool you've worked so hard on. So, your UI/UX design has to be intuitive, user-friendly, engaging, and offer a seamless experience. Key areas include information architecture and workflows, wireframes, style guides, mockups, and prototypes.

Step 4: App Development - Now that the design part is complete, it's time to move on to one of the most important stages of your mobile app implementation. This includes backend development (databases and server-side objects), frontend development (platform-specific, cross-platform, or hybrid approaches), and DevOps practices.

Step 5: Mobile App Testing - Once you're done with app development, it's time to ensure your app performs well. Only with quality assurance and software testing in place can you ensure launching a stable, secure, and bug-free application. Testing methods include functionality, performance, security, platform and device compatibility, regression testing, and extended beta review.

Step 6: Deployment - When you're finally ready to deploy your app, you'll have to go through different distribution models depending on the platform you were developing for. You'll either need to submit your application to Apple's App Store or Google's Play Store or go for private distribution. ASO tools assist with app keyword optimization and user review analysis to elevate your product's ranking.

Step 7: App Localization - When aiming to create a mobile app that will be accessible worldwide, localization has no alternative. This involves planning early, using Unicode, keeping translatable content separate from code, adopting different date/time/number/currency formats, designing flexible layouts, and testing the localized version.

Step 8: Support and Performance Monitoring - Once your app is finally live and users are steadily downloading it, it's time to get your analytical hat on and monitor performance. Some of the most important KPIs include app downloads, active users, average visit time, retention, churn, conversions, customer lifetime value, ratings, and reviews. Mobile apps require regular updates to keep up with the latest operating system changes.

Key Considerations Before Starting: Before setting aside a budget and embarking on a software development journey, consider the strategic relevance of building a mobile app, implications of developing enterprise vs. consumer-facing apps, and the choice between in-house development and outsourcing.
